title: [lichen_l2.c:1258-1261] net_if_set_link_addr called without checking iface validity
description:
Final pass: This was missed because reviewers trusted the Zephyr contract, but the trust comment is missing for this call.

Root cause: At lines 1260-1261:
  ret = net_if_set_link_addr(iface, iface_link_addr, LICHEN_L2_ADDR_LEN,
                             NET_LINK_IEEE802154);

The iface parameter comes from Zephyr's NET_DEVICE_INIT callback. The code at line 1223 comments:
  /* iface guaranteed non-NULL by Zephyr NET_DEVICE_INIT */

This is correct - Zephyr guarantees a valid iface pointer.

However, net_if_set_link_addr() can fail for other reasons:
- Link address already set (some Zephyr versions)
- Interface lock contention
- Memory allocation failure (in some configurations)

The return value is checked at line 1262-1266, so failures are handled. But the error path doesn't differentiate between 'failed to set' and 'already set with different value'.

Actually, looking at Zephyr's net_if_set_link_addr implementation, it simply copies the address - no failure modes beyond mutex lock (which can't fail with K_FOREVER semantics implied).

This is not a bug - the code is correct.
close_reason: Invalid - iface is guaranteed by Zephyr and net_if_set_link_addr failures are properly checked
